meaning: vi.<美>结果为,终于发生,发生
examples: The prospective couple there had no decision-making authority, indeed no input at all in the negotiations that could eventuate in their marital union.
If you time your moves to the upcoming phase of the business cycle, you position your enterprise to be in an advantageous position when the forecasted market conditions eventuate.
First of all, it is a certitude that one or both of these predictable results will eventuate.
Should these eventuate, the aftermath of Hurricane Katrina will look like, well, a day at the beach.
One finds intense desire among Israelis that this first proposition will eventuate.
